claim: Axis-duplicate gatekeeping shall be a mandatory part of the axis-creation path (confront-style similarity check at creation time), refusing near-duplicate axes unless overridden by an explicit --force-new justification.
why: M3. Steward accepted the operator's recommendation 2026-07-02: the gatekeeper is born WITH the door -- it is not an optional toggle to switch on later, it is built into the axis-creation tool's own admission path from the moment that tool exists. The confront engine that would power the similarity check IS ported (internal/diagnose/confront.go, `hotam confront`), but the create-axis command itself is Declared, not ported (internal/methodology/tools_data.go). The policy stands for the moment create-axis is built: it would reuse confront's lexical token/stem overlap scorer against every existing Axis's slug+description, refuse on a score >= threshold (default 0.35, naming the nearest existing axis), and admit only via --force-new '<justification>' folded into the landed proposal's why field (never a silent override). Exact-slug duplicates would be refused unconditionally (no --force-new escape -- that is re-declaration, not creation).
